DEFENDANT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM ON JUNE 8, 2012 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT DISCLOSE THAT IT IS NECESSARILY BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE REQUIRING EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE VEHICLE CODE SECTION 17001 AS THE BASIS OF HIS CLAIM. THERE IS NO NEED TO ALLEGE EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES IF THE LANGUAGE OF THE COMPLAINT IS NOT CHANGED OTHER THAN TO ALLEGE SECTION 17001 AS THE BASIS OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2012 Continued To Jul-25-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel B. Alweiss, DAG, from the Office of the Attorney General, 415-703-1276, for defendant and moving party
Thomas A. Ambrose, Esq., Law Office, 707-429-5550, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint does not disclose that it is necessarily barred by the doctrine requiring exhaustion of administrative remedies. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege Vehicle Code section 17001 as the basis of his claim. There is no need to allege exhaustion of administrative remedies if the language of the complaint is not changed other than to allege section 17001 as the basis of plaintiff's claim. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2012 Continued To Jun-14-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued on the court's own motion to June 14, 2012 because the court did not receive courtesy copies of the moving papers. Defendant must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to department 302 no later than 4pm on June 8, 2012 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2012 Continued To Jun-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Appearance for Plaintiff. The OSC is re-set to 6/18/2012 at 2:00 PM, in Department 610. Proceedings not reported. (610)
